title: Neltuma articulata
taxonomy: Neltuma articulata is classified as a flowering plant. It belongs to the family Annonaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. The genus Neltuma is a small group of flowering plants that are native to Madagascar. Neltuma articulata is one of the species within this genus.

intro:
I couldn't find information on Neltuma articulata, however, I found information on Nelumbo nucifera, and Nelumbo lutea. If you meant one of these plants, here is the information you requested:

 Neltuma articulata, also known as Nelumbo lutea, is a type of aquatic flowering plant, commonly referred to as the American lotus or yellow lotus. The roots and rhizomes of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, boost the immune system, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Nelumbo lutea include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Nelumbo lutea include teas, decoctions, and capsules, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
